mechanism_name: NAPE → NAE Biosynthesis from Dietary Phospholipids
summary: Dietary phosphatidylethanolamine and choline-supported phospholipid pathways that supply N-acyl phosphatidylethanolamines (NAPEs) and downstream N-acyl ethanolamines (NAEs) including PEA, OEA, and anandamide.
dose_sensitivity: Pattern-based choline and PE-rich food inclusion
functional_outputs: ↑ NAPE substrate availability; ↑ NAE biosynthesis support; ↑ endocannabinoid tone context
evidence_notes: NAPE/NAE biology is mechanistically established; human dietary dose-response for brain ECS tone remains context-dependent.

mechanistic_detail.summary: Endocannabinoid tone depends not only on receptor signalling but on whether dietary phospholipid chemistry can sustain NAPE and NAE production.

blocks.heading: (PE, NAPE, and NAE biosynthesis)
paragraphs: Phosphatidylethanolamine can be converted to N-acyl phosphatidylethanolamines (NAPEs), precursors to bioactive N-acyl ethanolamines including PEA, OEA, and anandamide → [Garani, Watts, and Mizrahi (2021)](/docs/papers/BRAIN-Diet-References#garani_endocannabinoid_2021) [1]

blocks.heading: (Boundaries of the mechanism)
paragraphs: Direct cholinergic acetylcholine synthesis is owned by BRS1-FM3-PM4. Membrane DHA incorporation and omega-3 NAE production are handled by BRS1-FM4-PM5 and BRS-X(ECS-PM3).

blocks.heading: (Integration within BRS-X(ECS))
paragraphs: This PM operationalises substrate supply within BRS-X(ECS-FM1) Endocannabinoid Tone Regulation, constrained by BRS-X(ECS-KC1).
